commit.message:
TLA+ Formal Specifications for BurnBuddy API (#28)

* feat: [US-001] - TLA+ project structure, Common module, and README

- Create specs/tla/ directory with subdirectories for each domain
- Create Common.tla shared module with Uid, Timestamp, Status, WorkoutType,
  WorkoutStatus types and type-invariant operators
- Create README.md with directory structure, TLC CLI usage, output
  interpretation, and TLA+ learning resources

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: [US-002] - Friend Management TLA+ spec

Model friend request lifecycle (send, accept, decline) and friendship
management (create via acceptance, delete). Verifies safety invariants:
- At most one pending request per direction between two users
- No self-requests
- No duplicate friendships (canonical sorted UID pairs)
- Friendship symmetry via unordered set representation

TLC verified: 14.4M states, 1.4M distinct, 0 violations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: [US-003] - Burn Buddy Management TLA+ spec

Model burn buddy request lifecycle (send/accept/decline) and relationship
management (update schedule/delete) with friendship dependency enforcement.

Invariants verified by TLC (26.3M states, 1.73M distinct, 0 violations):
- Pending buddy requests require active friendship
- At most one buddy relationship per user pair
- At most one pending request per direction
- No self buddy requests
- Friendship deletion cascades to pending buddy requests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-004 - Burn Squad Management TLA+ spec

Formal specification of burn squad lifecycle, membership, join requests,
and permission enforcement (onlyAdminsCanAddMembers setting).

Invariants verified:
- Squad always has exactly one admin who is always in memberUids
- Cannot invite non-friends
- Admin-only invite enforcement when restricted
- No self-invitations
- Unique squad IDs
- Pending join requests reference existing squads

TLC results: 8.9M states generated, 917K distinct states, 0 violations, depth 39

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-005 - Workout Lifecycle and Group Detection TLA+ spec

Models workout start/end lifecycle, auto-end stale workouts, and
concurrent group workout detection for both buddy pairs and squads.

Verified invariants:
- Completed workouts have endedAt set
- User can only end their own workouts (structural)
- At most one GroupWorkout per buddy/squad per detection window (dedup)
- GroupWorkout for buddy requires both members active within window
- GroupWorkout for squad requires ALL members active within window
- Detection does not block workout creation (structural)

TLC results: 155K states, 75K distinct, 0 violations, depth 15

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-006 - User Profile Management TLA+ spec

Model profile creation, username reservation, username changes, and
profile picture lifecycle. Verifies username uniqueness (case-insensitive),
atomic username changes, no orphaned reservations, and profile creation
idempotency.

TLC results: 15.7K states generated, 1.2K distinct states, 0 violations.
Config: 3 users, 4 usernames.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-007 - Push Notifications TLA+ spec

TLA+ specification for BurnBuddy push notification flows modeling the
fire-and-forget notification pattern triggered by workout creation.

Verifies 6 invariants with 0 violations (195K states, 69K distinct):
- ResponseNeverBlocked: notification failures never block HTTP response
- NoSelfNotification: user never receives own workout notification
- AllPartnersTargeted: all buddy partners and squad members targeted
- OnlyTokenUsersReceive: only FCM token holders get notifications
- NoTokenSkippedCleanly: missing tokens silently skipped (no error)
- TypeOK: all variables stay within expected domains

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-008 - Cross-Domain Invariants TLA+ spec

Composite spec verifying cross-domain safety properties:
- CDI-1: Profile required before social operations
- CDI-2: Friendship required before burn buddy creation
- CDI-3/4: GroupWorkout references/memberUids valid at creation
- CDI-5: Friend deletion prevents new buddy requests
- CDI-6/7: Buddy/squad deletion orphans GroupWorkouts (by design)

TLC verified: 38,135 states, 6,331 distinct, 0 errors.
Documents discrepancies: API friend handler lacks explicit profile
check; burn buddy/GroupWorkout cascade deletion is by-design absent.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: update PRD and progress for US-001 through US-008

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-009 - GitHub Actions TLC model-checking workflow

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: update PRD and progress for US-009

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-010 - API verification report mapping specs to implementation

Maps all 54 TLA+ invariants across 7 domain specs to their corresponding
API route handler code locations. Identifies 4 gaps:

- G-1 (Critical): Duplicate burn buddy per pair possible
- G-2 (Low): No explicit self-invite check in squad member add
- G-3 (Medium): Username uniqueness race condition
- G-4 (Critical): Profile not required for social actions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: [US-011] - Automated tests for gaps found in verification report

Add 8 tests documenting TLA+ specification gaps found in VERIFICATION_REPORT.md:

- G-1 (burn-buddies.test.ts): AtMostOneBuddyPerPair — verifies accept handler
  uses randomUUID doc ID (not sorted composite key) and doesn't check for
  existing BurnBuddy before creation, allowing duplicate pairs.

- G-2 (burn-squads.test.ts): NoSelfInvites — verifies POST /:id/members has no
  explicit self-invite check; prevention relies on friendship-with-self being
  impossible (cross-domain assumption).

- G-3 (users.test.ts): UsernameUniqueness — verifies generateUniqueUsername is
  called outside a Firestore transaction, creating a TOCTOU race condition
  where concurrent profile creations can claim the same username.

- G-4 (friends/burn-buddies/burn-squads/workouts .test.ts):
  ProfileRequiredForSocialActions (CDI-1) — verifies that none of the social
  action endpoints check for Firestore profile existence before proceeding.

Each test references the TLA+ spec and invariant name in comments.
All 337 tests pass (329 existing + 8 new).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Cross-platform USB (hidapi) and BLE (Qt Bluetooth) support

USB transport:
- Replace Linux hidraw with hidapi library (Linux/Windows/macOS)
- hid_open(VID=0x2e3c, PID=0x5558), hid_read_timeout() loop, no event loop needed
- Thread-safe sendCommand() via QMutex + hid_write()
- Write buffer needs 0x00 report-ID prefix as required by hidapi

BLE transport:
- Replace BlueZ D-Bus with Qt Bluetooth QLowEnergyController (cross-platform)
- BleTransport now takes QBluetoothDeviceInfo (required on macOS/Windows)
- Notifications via CCCD descriptor write (0100)
- sendCommand() writes to FFE9 write characteristic
- Guarded with FNB58_HAVE_BLUETOOTH; graceful stub if Qt Bluetooth not installed

Device scan:
- Replace bluetoothctl QProcess with QBluetoothDeviceDiscoveryAgent (5 s scan)
- Caches QBluetoothDeviceInfo by MAC in DeviceBackend for connect-by-address

CI:
- Linux: add libhidapi-dev, libbluetooth-dev; Qt module qtconnectivity
- macOS: brew install hidapi; Qt module qtconnectivity
- Windows: vcpkg install hidapi; Qt module qtconnectivity; VCPKG toolchain

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: optimize filter rendering - 37% faster (#146)

* perf: optimize filter rendering - 37% faster

Optimized box blur and filter pipeline for significant performance gains:

**Box Blur Optimizations (from JSVG)**:
- Use fixed-point arithmetic: scale = (1 << 24) / boxSize
- Check pixel equality to skip redundant calculations
- Combine add/subtract operations in single pass
- Use bit shifts for RGB channel extraction

**Filter Pipeline Optimizations**:
- Lazy buffer allocation (only allocate BufferedImage when needed)
- Direct pixel operations for dropShadow colorization (avoid Graphics2D)
- Direct alpha compositing for dropShadow output (avoid Graphics2D drawImage)

**Results**:
- Before: 21.6ms avg
- After: 13.7ms avg
- Improvement: 37% faster
- Gap vs JSVG: reduced from -156% to -60%

Addresses #136

commit.message:
Fix RAG model display: prefer actual data over stale entries

Three-layer fix for stale embedding model display after sync:

1. refreshRagSummary: Infer model from dims using sibling repos
   (e.g., tio-front-end has Qwen@1024 → all 1024-dim repos infer Qwen)

2. RepositoryAggregator: When merging repos with same identifier,
   prefer model/dims from entry with most chunks (actual data).
   Discards stale model from 0-chunk parent entries.

3. liveEmbeddingModel: Search ALL matching ragRepos (not just first)
   and pick the one with most chunks for display.

Root cause: Sub-packages synced from Mac Studio had NULL embedding_model
in DB (older sync code), while the stale local kitchen-sink entry had
'nomic' with 0 chunks. Previous code used first-match or nil-coalesce
which always picked the stale nomic model.

fix: 修复远程部署后教师后台看不到考试的问题

根本原因：
1. Coolify 可能不从 docker-compose.yaml 的 environment: 段读取 DOCS_DIR，
   导致容器内 DOCS_DIR=""，sync_exams() 跳过扫描，数据库无考试记录。
2. 若挂载卷 ./docs 失败，Docker 会在容器内创建空目录 /app/docs，
   原代码 found={} 时会删除数据库中所有已存在的考试（孤立清理逻辑有误）。

commit.message:
fix: null pointer deref in CbcMain1 when preProcessNonDefault returns null

Commit da489d33 added a 'double check' resolve() call to verify infeasibility
before retrying with simpler preprocessing. However, the enclosing condition
also handles the case where preProcessNonDefault returns null (preprocessing
itself detected infeasibility), and calling solver2->resolve() on a null pointer
causes a SIGSEGV.

Split the single condition into two explicit branches:
- Case A (solver2 == null): go directly to the simpler-preprocessing retry,
  which is the original pre-da489d33 behaviour for this path.
  resolve() introduced by da489d33, only retrying if resolve confirms
  infeasibility.

Add testInfeasiblePreprocess() to CInterfaceTest.c with the 8-variable /
14-constraint reproducer reported by the user, which exercises the Case A
null path that was previously triggering the crash.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

treated as Dog-typed inside the if-body, enabling direct struct field
access without manual `d: Dog = x` annotations. Supports negated
isinstance (narrows in else branch), elif chains, and method context.
